name: m16.3-b3-paro-ffn-megakernel-microbench
hardware: AMD Radeon Pro W7900 (gfx1100 / RDNA3), 48 CUs
model_shape: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B PARO: hidden=2048, moe_intermediate(ffn_len)=512, num_experts=256, top_k=8, group_size=128, krot=1 (synthetic AWQ weights)
workload: selected-expert MoE FFN at the verify/decode shape; c decode tokens -> rows=c*top_k selected (token,expert) blocks
fused_kernel: paro_selected_ffn_fused_kernel<unsigned short, unsigned short> (one block per (token,expert); rotate1 -> AWQ gate/up GEMV -> silu*mul -> down-rotate -> AWQ down GEMV; intermediate on-chip)
unfused_baseline: paro_rotate1 -> gemv_awq_selected_dual_pack8_transposed -> silu_mul_dual_rotate_out -> gemv_awq_selected_pack8_transposed (production PARO chain)

correctness_gate.guard: tests/test_paro_moe_ffn_fused.py 5 passed (f32 max_rel 1.8e-7 vs cpu_reference primitive recompute; bf16 KL<=0.05/top1>=0.90 vs paro_moe_selected_ffn oracle; row-invariance bit-exact; inactive->zeros)

kept_iterations: hoist per-group qzeros/scales + int4/float4 vectorize: 1.378 -> 0.556 ms
fuse gate+up dual-dot (share staged activation): 0.556 -> 0.351 ms
down stage thread-owns-pack (load each packed word once): 0.351 -> 0.249 ms

rocprof: single dispatch of paro_selected_ffn_fused_kernel<unsigned short,unsigned short>; grid 8192 work-items / 256 = 32 blocks (one per (token,expert)); VGPR_Count=136, Scratch_Size=0, SGPR=128
interpretation: Kernel-time half of C_B is now well past the B5 crossover: the fused PARO FFN megakernel is 4.1x faster than the unfused chain it replaces at the B=3 verify shape (c=4) and faster across c=1..16, single-launch, no scratch, correctness held. This is a microbench A/B (one FFN call), not an end-to-end C_B claim. Next: B4 wire the fused key into runtime/moe_c1_dispatch.py and measure the actual verify-cycle C_B delta on the real model.
